original.title: 65 Doctors, Nurses and Paramedics: What We Saw in Gaza
original.summary: New York Times interactive feature presents testimonies from doctors who worked in Gaza during the conflict.

signals.page_title: Opinion | What Doctors and Health Care Workers in Gaza Saw - The New York Times
signals.text_snippet: SKIP TO CONTENT SKIP TO SITE INDEX Section Navigation Search LOG IN OPINION GUEST ESSAY 65 Doctors, Nurses and Paramedics: What We Saw in Gaza Daniel Benneworth-Gray By Feroze Sidhwa Dr. Sidhwa is a trauma and general surgeon who worked at the European Hospital in Khan Younis, Gaza, for two weeks in March and April. Oct. 9, 2024 Share full article 440 I worked as a trauma surgeon in Gaza from March 25 to April 8. I’ve volunteered in Ukraine and Haiti, and I grew up in Flint, Mich. I’ve seen violence and worked in conflict zones. But of the many things that stood out about working in a hospital in Gaza, one got to me: Nearly every day I was there, I saw a new young child who had been shot in the head or the chest, virtually all of whom went on to die. Thirteen in total. At the time, I assumed this had to be the work of a particularly sadistic soldier located nearby. But after returning ho

signals.text_snippet: Skip to main content The homepage Explainers Politics Culture Advice Listen Watch SIGN IN BECOME A MEMBER MENU Is Israel committing genocide? Reexamining the question, a year later. The context you need, when you need it When news breaks, you need to understand what actually matters — and what to do about it. At Vox, our mission to help you make sense of the world has never been more vital. But we can’t do it on our own. We rely on readers like you to fund our journalism. Will you support our work and become a Vox Member today? Join now POLITICS Is Israel committing genocide? Reexamining the question, a year later. We spoke to a number of experts a year ago. original.title: Amos Goldberg: 'What is happening in Gaza is a genocide because Gaza does not exist anymore'
original.summary: Le Monde publishes opinion piece by Amos Goldberg arguing that the situation in Gaza constitutes genocide.

signals.page_title: Amos Goldberg: 'What is happening in Gaza is a genocide because Gaza does not exist anymore'
signals.text_snippet: Date: Saturday, May 9, 2026 Time: 11:08 am (Paris) Go Back to the HomePage Le Monde Go Back to the HomePage Le Monde FR EN Subscribe Menu Search Search War in Gaza Trump's plan explained Five scenarios for the future States recognize Palestine The genocide question OPINION OPINION OPINION WAR IN GAZA WAR IN GAZA WAR IN GAZA Amos Goldberg: 'What is happening in Gaza is a genocide because Gaza does not exist anymore' Given the scale of the massacres and destruction perpetrated by the Israeli army in Gaza, the Israeli historian, a specialist of the Holocaust, says in an interview with Le Monde that his country 'criminally overreacted' to the October 7 massacres. Interview by Stéphanie Le Bars Published on October 29, 2024, at 11:27 am (Paris) 8 min read Lire en français Subscribers only Amos Goldberg, Israeli historian.
